orientation.description: Canvas orientation: 'vertical' = 9:16 1080×1920 (default, shorts/reels), 'horizontal' = 16:9 1920×1080 (YouTube). Every frame type supports both.

width.description: Explicit canvas width; overrides the orientation default

height.description: Explicit canvas height; overrides the orientation default

audio.description: Single full-script narration path relative to videos/<slug>/

voiceStyle.description: Metadata for LLM pipeline; not used at render

capHighlight.description: Karaoke highlight color (hex) for the caption word currently being spoken; independent of per-frame accent. Default #FFD24A (gold).

id.description: Unique kebab-case id → filename and composition-id

type.description: Frame type selector. All types render in both orientations at the canvas size resolved from config.orientation.

typeUsage.cover: DEFAULT for the opening scene / topic intro hook. A safe general choice.
typeUsage.stats: use when the scene centers on ONE striking number, percentage, or figure.
typeUsage.quote: use ONLY when the narration is a pull quote, bold claim, or citation. Shows quote text and attribution styled as a code comment.
typeUsage.bullet-list: DEFAULT for general explanatory content — a titled list of 3-5 points/steps/skills with no dedicated visual. A safe general choice when nothing more specific fits.
typeUsage.cta: DEFAULT for the closing scene / takeaway / call to action.
typeUsage.concept-card: use when the narration defines ONE named term or concept (e.g. 'an embedding is…', 'an agent is…'). Term + tagline + short definition card.
typeUsage.code-snippet: use ONLY when the narration walks through actual code. Shows an editor window revealing code line by line with key lines highlighted.
typeUsage.terminal: use ONLY when the narration is about running commands / CLI tools (install, deploy, run). Shows a terminal typing commands and printing output. Fits 1-3 short interactions.
typeUsage.chat: use ONLY when the narration describes a prompt/response exchange with an LLM or agent. Shows user/assistant chat bubbles. Fits 2-4 short messages.
typeUsage.pipeline: use when the narration describes a system as a FLOW of stages (e.g. RAG: query → retrieve → augment → generate). Boxes-and-arrows with an animated pulse traveling the path.
typeUsage.comparison: use ONLY when directly contrasting two named approaches/technologies (RAG vs fine-tuning, SQL vs NoSQL). Two panels with trait rows and a VS badge.
typeUsage.roadmap: use when the narration describes ordered milestones over time or a learning path (path to AI engineer, model evolution). Numbered nodes along a drawn line.
typeUsage.stack-layers: use when the narration describes layered architecture (UI / orchestration / model / infra) and especially when highlighting WHERE something sits in the stack.
typeUsage.vector-space: use ONLY when the narration is about embeddings, vector similarity, or retrieval/nearest-neighbor search. Animated 2D embedding scatter with a query point and top-k matches.
typeUsage.neural-net: use ONLY when the narration is about neural networks / how models process signals through layers. Animated layered network with signal pulses.
typeUsage.task-breakdown: use ONLY when the narration is about an agent decomposing a big goal into smaller steps / planning a to-do list. Goal card fans out into a checklist that ticks itself.
typeUsage.thought-chain: use ONLY when the narration is about chain-of-thought / the agent reasoning step by step before answering. Question pill → thinking cards → answer card.
typeUsage.tool-use: use ONLY when the narration is about an agent calling external tools (web search, calculator, database, APIs). Agent hub with tool spokes and request/response pulses.
typeUsage.memory: use ONLY when the narration contrasts or explains agent memory — short-term conversation context vs long-term preferences/rules. Two stores fill up, then items are recalled.
typeUsage.reflection-loop: use ONLY when the narration is about self-reflection / the agent checking its own work, catching an error and retrying. Cycle ring: first lap fails at the check, second lap passes.
typeUsage.mcp-hub: use ONLY when the narration is about Model Context Protocol or standardized integrations — one plug connecting the agent to many external apps. Agent → MCP hub → app chips.
typeUsage.photo: use when the scene is best shown with a REALISTIC PHOTOGRAPH filling the screen (a real workspace, hardware, a person coding) rather than a diagram. Write a vivid 'imagePrompt'; the system generates the picture, which is ANIMATED (add an optional 'anim' to pick the camera move — slow zoom, pan, focus-pull, breathe — matching the scene's mood). Prefer the schematic tech types when the content is a diagram/flow.
typeUsage.photo-split: like 'photo' but pairs the generated (animated) photograph with a short block of text (headline + body) beside it — use when you want a realistic image AND a sentence of explanation on screen together. Optional 'anim' picks the picture's motion.

anim.description: Optional motion spec for an IMAGE frame (photo, photo-split) — how the generated picture moves. Omit to accept a gentle ken-burns-in push. Choose values that fit the scene's mood.

preset.description: Camera move across the still. ken-burns-in/out = slow zoom in/out; pan-* = drift across the frame (good for establishing/landscape shots); push-diagonal = zoom + diagonal drift; focus-pull = starts blurred then snaps sharp (a reveal); breathe = very subtle living pulse.

focus.description: Where the move anchors (transform-origin / point of interest). Default 'center'.

overlay.description: Optional atmosphere layer on top of the image: sweep = a light glint crossing once; vignette = breathing dark edges; grain = subtle film grain; glow = accent edge glow. Default 'none'.

start.description: Scene start in seconds (hand-authored until a caption-alignment pipeline is wired in).

bg.description: Full-bleed background color (hex). Default is the dark developer canvas #0D1117 — override sparingly.

eyebrow.description: Small mono badge above the headline, ≤24 chars, e.g. '~/ai-engineering' or 'EP.01 — RAG'

headline.description: Main display text, ≤44 chars; wrap key words in **double asterisks** for accent highlight

captions.description: Naturally-split caption chunks displayed sequentially during the scene, 2-6 words and ≤55 chars each. Doubles as the scene's expected narration text (chunks joined with spaces).

captionTiming.description: Populate/align-computed, not hand-authored: one {text,start,end,words} per captions[] chunk in scene-local seconds. words[] holds one {text,start,end} per whitespace token for the karaoke spoken-word highlight — real Whisper timestamps from align-captions.mjs, or evenly spaced within the chunk span when populate.js synthesizes them. If captionTiming is absent, populate.js evenly spaces captions[] across the scene duration.

stat.description: Large stat figure, digits/symbols only, ≤5 chars, e.g. '80' or '10x' — count-up animates numeric parts (stats)

statLabel.description: Short label under the stat, ≤44 chars, e.g. 'of AI projects never ship' (stats)

prefix.description: Small text before the stat, e.g. '$' or '~' — optional (stats)

suffix.description: Small text after the stat, e.g. '%' or 'ms' — optional (stats)

attribution.description: Who said it / source, ≤36 chars, rendered as a mono comment line (quote)

title.description: Short heading label above the visual, ≤26 chars — meaning per type: list title (bullet-list), conversation topic (chat), diagram name e.g. 'RAG PIPELINE' (pipeline), path name e.g. 'PATH TO AI ENGINEER' (roadmap), stack name (stack-layers), plot name e.g. 'EMBEDDING SPACE' (vector-space), network name (neural-net)

items.description: 3-5 short point strings revealed sequentially, ≤38 chars each; wrap key words in ** for accent (bullet-list)

subheadline.description: Secondary line under the headline, one short sentence ≤60 chars (cta)

term.description: The concept being defined, 1-3 words ≤18 chars, e.g. 'Embedding' (concept-card)

tagline.description: Short mono subtitle under the term, ≤26 chars, e.g. 'text → numbers' — optional (concept-card)

definition.description: One-sentence plain-language definition, ≤110 chars; wrap key words in ** for accent (concept-card)

glyph.description: Single emoji/character badge for the concept, e.g. '🧭' — optional (concept-card)

filename.description: Filename shown in the editor tab, e.g. 'rag.py' (code-snippet)

language.description: Language hint for the syntax highlighter: python, javascript, typescript, bash, json (code-snippet)

code.description: Code as an array of line strings (4-10 lines, keep each ≤46 chars so it fits both orientations). Revealed line by line. (code-snippet)

highlightLines.description: 1-based line numbers to sweep the accent highlight bar onto after the reveal — the 'look here' moment (code-snippet)

commands.description: 1-3 terminal interactions: each {cmd, output[]} — cmd is typed character by character, output lines cascade in. Keep cmd and every output line ≤34 chars, and total lines (cmds + outputs) ≤9. (terminal)

messages.description: 2-4 chat messages: {role: 'user'|'assistant', text}. User bubbles enter right, assistant left with typing indicator then word-by-word reveal. Keep each text ≤72 chars and the whole conversation ≤290 chars. (chat)

nodes.description: 3-5 pipeline stages in flow order: {label, sublabel?, glyph?} — label 1-2 words ≤12 chars (e.g. 'Vector DB'), sublabel a tiny mono annotation ≤15 chars (e.g. 'top-k docs'), glyph a single emoji. (pipeline)

highlightNode.description: 0-based index of the node to accent-glow as the focus of the scene; -1/omit for none (pipeline)

leftItems.description: 2-3 short trait rows for the left/top panel, ≤28 chars each (comparison)

rightItems.description: 2-3 short trait rows for the right/bottom panel, ≤28 chars each (comparison)

verdict.description: Short concluding line shown after both panels, ≤60 chars, e.g. 'Use both.' — optional (comparison)

steps.description: Ordered steps — meaning per type: for roadmap, 3-5 milestones {label, sublabel?} (label 1-4 words ≤20 chars, sublabel a tiny detail line ≤21 chars); for reflection-loop, 3-4 cycle stages {label, glyph?} (label ≤9 chars, e.g. Attempt ✍️ / Check 🔎 / Fix 🔧 — the SECOND step is treated as the check point). (roadmap, reflection-loop)

layers.description: 3-5 stack layers TOP-first: {label, sublabel?} — label ≤18 chars, sublabel ≤25 chars, e.g. [{label:'UI'},{label:'Orchestration'},{label:'Model'},{label:'Infra'}]. (stack-layers)

highlightIndex.description: 0-based index of the layer to highlight with glow + annotation callout; -1/omit for none (stack-layers)

annotation.description: Short callout text pointing at the highlighted layer, ≤22 chars, e.g. 'you work HERE' (stack-layers)

clusterLabels.description: 2-3 cluster names for the embedding scatter, e.g. ['docs about cats','docs about code'] — keep each ≤19 chars (vector-space)

queryLabel.description: Label on the query point that drops in, ≤14 chars, e.g. 'your question' (vector-space)

layerLabels.description: Optional labels for input/hidden/output layers, e.g. ['tokens','layers','next word'] — max 3, each ≤12 chars (neural-net)

outputLabel.description: Text shown at the output side once signals arrive, ≤13 chars, e.g. 'prediction' — optional (neural-net)

goal.description: The big goal being decomposed, one short phrase ≤28 chars shown on the goal card, e.g. 'Plan a product launch' (task-breakdown)

subtasks.description: 3-5 checklist steps the goal breaks into: {label, sublabel?} — label 2-5 words ≤26 chars, sublabel a tiny mono detail ≤19 chars. Checked off sequentially. (task-breakdown)

question.description: The prompt the agent is reasoning about, ≤40 chars, shown in the top pill, e.g. 'Should we refund order #482?' (thought-chain)

thoughts.description: 2-4 short internal reasoning steps, first person, e.g. 'My goal is to check the policy first.' Keep each ≤70 chars. (thought-chain)

conclusion.description: The final decision/answer card text, ≤80 chars; wrap key words in ** for accent (thought-chain)

agentLabel.description: Label on the agent node, 1-3 words ≤13 chars, e.g. 'Support Agent' (tool-use, mcp-hub)

tools.description: 3-5 tools the agent can call: {glyph, label, sublabel?} — glyph a single emoji (🔍 🧮 🗄️), label 1-2 words ≤12 chars, sublabel ≤14 chars. (tool-use)

shortLabel.description: Heading for the short-term store — defaults to 'SHORT-TERM' (memory)

longLabel.description: Heading for the long-term store — defaults to 'LONG-TERM' (memory)

shortItems.description: 2-3 recent-context snippets, e.g. 'User asked about order #482' — keep each ≤36 chars (memory)

longItems.description: 2-3 persistent preferences/rules, e.g. 'Prefers email replies' — keep each ≤36 chars (memory)

failLabel.description: Short status shown when the first pass fails the check, ≤12 chars, e.g. 'error found' — defaults to 'error found' (reflection-loop)

passLabel.description: Short status shown when the retry passes, ≤12 chars, e.g. 'fixed' — defaults to 'fixed' (reflection-loop)

hubLabel.description: Label on the protocol hub — defaults to 'MCP' (mcp-hub)

apps.description: 2-5 external apps the hub connects to: {glyph, label} — glyph a single emoji, label 1-2 words ≤10 chars, e.g. {glyph:'💬', label:'Slack'}. (mcp-hub)

imagePrompt.description: REQUIRED for photo / photo-split. A vivid, specific description of the photograph to generate for this scene (subject, setting, lighting, style) — the system generates the image from it. Describe a realistic photo; do NOT include text/words in the image. (photo, photo-split)

image.description: System-generated — DO NOT author this. Filled by the image-generation step with the rendered picture (a data URI); defaults to a placeholder. (photo, photo-split)

body.description: One short supporting sentence shown beside the image. Optional. (photo-split)
